description:
Discovered while fixing mutagen-de1 (coverage_runner_test r7 tmp-leak flake).

The r11 "no disk writes" test in test/mutagen_ex/mutation_runner_test.exs:1147
is the structural twin of the coverage r7 test: same DiskSnapshot helper, same
async: false, same four assertions, and the same global-System.tmp_dir!() +
umbrella `mutagen_ex` prefix attribution (mutagen_attributable_tmp/1).

It has the identical order/timing-dependent flake: a `mutagen_ex_`-prefixed tmp
entry from another lane (C1 :spike, archive install, baseline/beam_cache/memory)
or a concurrent process, churning through global tmp during the snapshot window,
is misattributed to the stubbed MutationRunner pass (which writes no tmp).

Reproduced deterministically-ish: `mix test --seed 78` surfaced
mutation_runner_test.exs:1147 once during the mutagen-de1 repro campaign (rare,
timing-sensitive).

Fix: mirror the mutagen-de1 fix — snapshot the tmp surface against a private
per-test directory instead of global System.tmp_dir!(); keep the disk-content
(lib/_build/cover/host) assertions global. See the resolved mutagen-de1 change
to coverage_runner_test.exs for the exact pattern and rationale, including the
deterministic regression guard.

Out of scope for mutagen-de1 (different test file); filed so it is not lost.
title: mutation_runner_test r11 shares the coverage r7 global-tmp flake (mutagen-de1 sibling)
